problem.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
problem.description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array. Note that it is the kth largest element in the sorted order, not the kth distinct element.
assumptions: Find kth largest in the array (not distinct kth largest).

example.explanation: 2nd largest is 5. Use min-heap of size k or QuickSelect for O(n) average.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Quick Select [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Apply the QuickSelect algorithm, which adapts the partitioning step of QuickSort to recurse only into the half containing the target index (nums.length - k in sorted order). After partitioning around a pivot, if the pivot lands exactly at the target index the answer is found; otherwise recurse left or right accordingly. The key insight is that unlike full sorting, QuickSelect discards one partition at each step, giving O(n) average time with O(1) extra space.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Min-Heap of Size K [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain a min-heap of size k. Push each element; if the heap exceeds k, pop the smallest. After processing all elements, the heap's minimum is the kth largest. O(n log k) time — preferred over QuickSelect when k is small or the stream is online.
